channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This slick new service puts ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Wikipedia on the map
item.description: I don’t know about you, but I tend to think about my favorite tech tools as being split into two separate saucepans: the “classic” apps we’ve known and relied on for ages and then the newer “AI” apps that have shown up over the past several months to serve some super-specific purpose.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: The next wave of AI is here: Autonomous AI agents are amazing—and scary
item.description: The relentless hype around AI makes it difficult to separate the signal from the noise. So it’s understandable if you’ve tuned out recent talk about autonomous AI agents. A word of advice: Don’t. The significance of agentic AI may actually exceed the hype.
item.dc:creator: Tom Barnett, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s hidden white noise feature may be just the productivity boost you need
item.description: As I write this, the most pleasing sound is washing over me—gentle waves ebbing and flowing onto the shore. Sadly, I’m not actually on some magnificent tropical beach. Instead, the sounds of the sea are being generated by my Mac.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Arizona advances bill to keep AI from rejecting medical claims
item.description: Arizona state lawmakers advanced a bill Thursday that would ban the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to deny medical claims. The Arizona House of Representatives passed the legislation 58-0, with two lawmakers sitting out the vote, and it now heads to the state senate for consideration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Amazon Surpasses Walmart in Quarterly Revenue Amid Holiday Season Boom
item.description: E-commerce giant Amazon reports $8 billion higher quarterly revenue than retail behemoth Walmart, despite being three times larger, due to a solid holiday season 2024 performance and continued struggles with high costs of physical stores.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: Apple says it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Substack bets big on video as TikTok’s future remains uncertain
item.description: With TikTok’s future in the U.S. still uncertain, Substack is doubling down on attracting video creators. As of yesterday, creators can now publish video posts directly from the Substack app—a feature previously limited to desktop.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Everything new on Max in March 2024
item.description: Here's the full list of everything new on Max in March 2025 – including new movie releases Sing Sing and Heretic.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft releases a new genAI model that can control robots
item.description: Microsoft has launched Magma, a new generative AI (genAI) model that can be used to control everything from software to robots. The company says Magma is the first such model that can act on its own based on multimodal data such as texts, images, and video.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Optoma’s tiny, portable ultra short throw laser projector can throw a 100-inch image from just 25cm away, wherever you take it
item.description: Optoma’s latest ultra short throw projector, the Photon Go, crams a whole lot of technology into a very small (and portable) package.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The most powerful Rolls-Royce in history is electric and it’s here to boost performance –and the marque's luxury appeal
item.description: Rolls-Royce's debut electric vehicle gets the Black Badge treatment, with 'murdered out' styling and a hefty dose of extra power.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why enterprises fail at finops
item.description: As cloud spending continues to climb, the discipline known as finops (financial operations) has emerged as a critical strategy for managing and optimizing cloud investments. This is no longer news. Finops has been a discipline and a toolkit for years.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump’s tariffs on China could risk access to rare earth elements widely used in tech sector
item.description: Lithium and other elements that the U.S. needs for vital defense, energy, and other applications could become harder to obtain if the Trump administration goes ahead with tariffs and other protectionist policies aimed at China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Musk calls former International Space Station commander 'idiot' in exchange over astronauts
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk called a former commander of the International Space Station an “idiot” on Thursday after he pushed back on Musk’s claim that the Biden administration “left” two astronauts in space “for political reasons.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump keeps cutting election security jobs. Here’s what’s at risk
item.description: As the Trump administration continues to dismantle federal agencies, one that plays a critical role in U.S. infrastructure and election security faces an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Bannon: ‘Oligarchs’ will abandon Republicans
item.description: Steve Bannon argued that the “oligarchs” close to President Trump will abandon Republicans the same way they did to Democrats. Bannon was briefly interviewed by CNN’s Donie O’Sullivan on Wednesday, when he continued his case against tech billionaires Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft’s new AI hub highlights apps that support NPUs
item.description: Applications that support the specialized AI chips on Copilot+ PCs have been rare, but Microsoft is looking to change that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: C# Dev Kit Updates Boost Coding Efficiency - Microsoft Enhancements
item.description: Microsoft updates C# Dev Kit for Visual Studio Code extension with enhancements to Razor/Blazor Hot Reload and debugging tools, boosting coding efficiency and productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future
item.description: A recent challenge sponsored by NIST tasked drone designers with creating a system to help first responders map indoor environments. The post How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Want to transform networking? Empower the missing users
item.description: As 2025 kicked off, I wrote a column about the network vendor landscape – specifically, which networking players will step up and put their efforts into finding new applications with solid business benefits that could enable a network transformation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Key strategies for MLops success in 2025
item.description: Integrating and managing artificial intelligence and machine learning effectively within business operations has become a top priority for businesses looking to stay competitive in an ever evolving landscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11
